843's engines cool the best if running unloaded and at slightly elevated RPM. If you find a reasonably long stretch of line without incline (such as at Sychrov and Hodkovice stations), you can switch the cruise control off, put the reverser to Diesel, and rev the engine to something like 1200-ish RPM. At the end of the level section, return RPM to idle, reverser to Forward, wait for RPM to drop, and continue your cruising.
A class 843 has broken down in Turnov. Can You fix it and drive it to Liberec for maintenance?
A moment, I will find the orders.

All right. You'll shunt from 10a towards Chrastava and stop just beyond the Se32 signal. Then you'll shunt to the 114th track where you'll finish shunting. Wait for the signaller of the fifth signalbox to allow the shunting.

And it is here! 95 degrees Celsius. At this temperature, the engine is no longer able to provide full power. With growing temperature, the power will be increasingly limited. Use every possible mean to cool the engine. The timetable is no longer a priority. The priority is to get to Jeřmanice - the summit of the line.

But your vacation is over and you are here - in the sweaty cab of the 843 class DMU. And the cab air cooler gave up! It is not even half past ten in the morning, and it is 38 degrees in shade already. Your engines are running hot, and so are you. Don't forget that the primary goal is to arrive safely!

Here is the brain of this vehicle, the central computer and traction regulator. As the Class 843 trains were created between years 1995 and 1997, some of the components still use analog control circuits. Here also lies the GM15 signal converter, which converts all signals between their analog and digital representation. Below you can find some important breakers, which control diesel engines and traction engines.

Here we see three switches, the left most one is disconnected, the middle one controls the operating mode. If you need to control another locomotive in double traction put it in the Double position. The last switch turns power regulation on or off. The locomotive won't apply any power if that switch is in the Off position.

Hold the Throttle and Brake lever in the S position. In this position you can increase the set power to get the train moving. You can see the set power on the display going between -100 %, 0 % and 100 %. The power will increase as long as you hold the lever in this position, after returning the lever to the J position the set power will be maintained. You can also decrease the set power in the V position. Decrease the set power to under 30 % as the speed reaches 55 km/h and then apply the power again.

In a similar way you can also control the Electrodynamic brake. Decrease the power back to 0 % and then hold the Throttle and Brake lever in BE position to increase the braking power. Set braking power can be then decreased in the J position.

Now look at the relays. The first important set are relays RCB, RD and RE, those three relays control which power notch the locomotive will use. Older Czech locomotives used binary or Grey codes to control the power.

Now that we have started the engine let's power up the radio and safety system. Turn on both J15 and RDST breakers. Also apply the direct brake to the maximum level. You can start the safety system when you have more than 2 bars of pressure in the brake cylinders.

Oh, well. This machine seems to feel more overheated than I do. It is better to reduce motoring to a necessary rate and let it glide downhill. The timetable is lazy enough. I hope it won't bring us much delay. On the other hand, every degree counts before the long and steep incline from Turnov to Rychnov.

The Engine Selection Switch decides which engine to start. The Class 843 has two diesel engines and this way you can start or shut them down. Positions in the left controls this vehicle and positions in the right control other vehicles in the train. Start by setting the switch to left position 1.

The Engine Selection Switch decides which engine to start. The Class 843 has two diesel engines and this way you can start or shut them down. Positions in the left controls this vehicle and positions in the right control other vehicles in the train. Start by setting the switch to left position 1.

The banking engines know about track speed restrictions, so in most cases you shouldn't have to worry about them pushing the train to greater speeds than allowed. However it is good practice to tell them to stop applying power before you start to brake. Don't worry if you get any of the signals wrong. You can just wait a few seconds and then try that signal again.

The banking engines know about track speed restrictions, so in most cases you shouldn't have to worry about them pushing the train to greater speeds than allowed. However it is good practice to tell them to stop applying power before you start to brake. Don't worry if you get any of the signals wrong. The breakers can trip the power to some components on this locomotive. As we've discussed previously, the two most important ones are the J15 (VZ) and RDST breakers. The J15 powers the LVZ safety system and RDST powers the radio. If any of those breakers are tripped you need to close the yellow valve in the engine room.

The breakers can trip the power to some components on this locomotive. As we've discussed previously, the two most important ones are the J15 (VZ) and RDST breakers. The J15 powers the LVZ safety system and RDST powers the radio. If any of those breakers are tripped you need to close the yellow valve in the engine room.

The last important relay is the RP. This relay controls field diversion which happens at around 60 km/h, but it is decided by generator voltage and current, so it can also happen at higher speeds if you have lower notches selected.

The second set of relays are relays RS1, RS2 and RS3. Those are the wheelslip relays. Regulation checks the voltage difference between traction engines and if any of those are big enough it will trip said relay. The locomotive would then automatically lower power until the wheelslip stops.

The yellow box with the valve is the safety system valve. If the safety system or radio aren't powered, air will leak through this valve. If you need to stop it and can't turn on any of those, then close the valve.

There are a few audible signals used for banking operations in Czechia. These can either be honks of the horn or blasts on the whistle. The most important is three short signals which tell the banking locomotives to stop applying any power.

There are a few audible signals used for banking operations in Czechia. These can either be honks of the horn or blasts on the whistle. The most important is three short signals which tell the banking locomotives to stop applying any power.

There are two ways how to tell the banking engines how to apply power. First one is one long and one short signals, which tells the engines to apply half power. Second is one long and two short signals, which tells the engines to apply maximum available power.

This is the safety system box. This locomotive is equipped with an LVZ safety system, which is responsible for checking driver's vigilance and also displays signal code, the latter applies only on routes that are equipped with said system.

Those electro-pneumatic valves belong to the BPE - direct brake controller. Valves YV70 and YV71 are braking and releasing valves. Valves YV81 and YV83 control the parking brake, valves YV82 and YV84 then control the complementary brake, which applies in case the electrodynamic brake isn't strong enough.

Those electro-pneumatic valves belong to the BPE - direct brake controller. Valves YV70 and YV71 are braking and releasing valves. Valves YV81 and YV83 control the parking brake, valves YV82 and YV84 then control the complementary brake, which applies in case the electrodynamic brake isn't strong enough.

Those electro-pneumatic valves belong to the BSE - indirect brake controller. Valves YB and YR are braking valves where YR controls the emergency braking. Valve YO is for releasing, YZ controls brake cutout and YS is for the quick release.
